source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A ruby laser emits light with a wavelength of $6943 \AA$, which to a very good approximation is a plane wave. What is the coherence length of the laser in vacuum (i.e., the distance over which the light stays coherent to $1 / 4$ of a wavelength) if the bandwidth of the laser is $\Delta \nu=30$ megahertz?

metadata.reasoning_chain:
The uncertainty principle states that $$\Delta t \cdot \Delta 
u \sim 1$$ where $\Delta 
u$ is the width of the spectrum line of the laser light, $\Delta t$ is the time of coherence. Thus the length of coherence is $$\Delta l= rac{c}{\Delta 
u}= rac{3 	imes 10^{8}}{30 	imes 10^{6}}=10 \mathrm{~m}$$
